info.title: Appstrate API
info.description:
API for Appstrate — an open-source platform for running autonomous AI agents in sandboxed Docker containers. Manage agents, runs, schedules, providers, API keys, and more.

## Common Response Headers

All API responses include a `Request-Id` header (`req_` prefix) for tracing. All authenticated responses additionally include an `Appstrate-Version` header with the resolved API version (format: `YYYY-MM-DD`). Deprecated API versions include a `Sunset` header (RFC 8594). Rate-limited endpoints return `RateLimit` and `RateLimit-Policy` headers on every response (not just 429).

tags.name: API Keys
tags.description: API key management for programmatic access

tags.name: Packages
tags.description: Organization skills, tools, and package management

tags.name: Application Packages
tags.description: Manage packages installed in an application

tags.name: Application Providers
tags.description: Manage provider credentials at the application level

get.description: Returns platform health status. No authentication required.

503.description: Platform unhealthy (database or critical service down)

post.description: Create a new account with email, password, and name. Sets session cookie.
Login with email and password. Sets session cookie.
Clears session cookie.

get.description: Returns the current session and user info.
Returns all agents (system + user-imported) with running run counts. Requires `X-Org-Id` header for cookie auth.

agents.description: Automatically sorts and labels incoming emails
agents.author: Acme Corp

agents.description: Reviews pull requests and suggests improvements
agents.author: Appstrate

put.description: Save agent configuration values. Validated against manifest config schema.

get.description: Returns the proxy configuration for an agent (override ID and resolution status).

put.description: Set a proxy override for this agent. Pass a proxy ID, "none" to disable proxying, or null to use org default.

proxyId.description: Proxy ID, "none" to opt out, or null for org default

get.description: Returns accumulated memories for an agent (org-scoped, shared across all users).

delete.description: Delete all accumulated memories for an agent.
Delete a specific memory by ID.

get.description: Returns the LLM model override for an agent (null if using org default).

put.description: Set a model override for this agent. Pass a model ID or null to revert to org default.

modelId.description: Model ID or null to use org default

put.description: Set the skill references for a user agent.
Set the tool references for a user agent.

get.description: Returns the per-provider connection profile overrides for an agent. Each key is a provider ID mapped to a profile ID.

overrides.description: Map of provider ID to profile ID

put.description: Override the connection profile used for a specific provider in this agent.

delete.description: Remove the connection profile override for a specific provider, reverting to the default profile.

put.summary: Set app profile for an agent
put.description: Set or clear the application-level connection profile for this agent. Pass a profile ID to set, or null to clear.

appProfileId.description: App profile ID or null to clear

post.description: Start an agent run (fire-and-forget). Returns the run ID. Rate-limited to 20/min. Supports JSON body or multipart/form-data with file uploads.

parameters.name: version
parameters.description: Version query to execute (exact version, dist-tag, or semver range). When provided, the run uses the versioned manifest and prompt instead of the live agent.

modelId.description: Model ID override for this run. Takes priority over agent and org defaults.

proxyId.description: Proxy ID override for this run, or "none" to disable proxying. Takes priority over agent and org defaults.

400.description: Agent readiness validation failed (empty prompt, missing skill/tool, provider not connected, or incomplete config)

get.description: List runs for a specific agent (org-scoped, default limit 50).

delete.summary: Delete all runs for an agent
delete.description: Delete all completed runs for an agent.

example.title: Conflict
example.detail: Cannot delete runs while agent has active runs

get.description: Get run details including status, result, input, and duration.
Get persisted log entries for a run.

post.description: Cancel a running or pending run.

example.title: Conflict
example.detail: Run has already completed and cannot be cancelled

get.description:
Server-Sent Events stream for all run status changes in the org. Supports cookie auth and API key auth via ?token=ask_... query parameter.

Event format: `event: run.status\ndata: {"id":"run_...","status":"running","packageId":"@scope/name",...}\n\n`

Event types: `run.status` (status change), `run.log` (log entry, single-run stream only). Heartbeat `:ping` every 30s.
Server-Sent Events stream for run status + log events. Supports cookie auth and API key auth via ?token=ask_... query parameter.
Server-Sent Events stream for run changes for a specific agent. Supports cookie auth and API key auth via ?token=ask_... query parameter.
List all schedules across all agents for the organization.
List all cron schedules configured for a specific agent.

post.description: Create a cron schedule for an agent.

connectionProfileId.description: Connection profile to use for provider credentials (user or app profile)

400.description: Validation error. Possible causes: missing/invalid cron expression, invalid input, or agent has file inputs (cannot be scheduled).

get.description: Get a single schedule by ID.

put.description: Update a cron schedule (expression, timezone, enabled state, or input).

delete.description: Permanently delete a cron schedule.

get.description: List recent runs triggered by a specific schedule.
List active provider connections for the current user in the org.

parameters.name: profileId
parameters.description: Connection profile ID (defaults to user's default profile)

get.summary: List all providers with connection status
get.description: List all configured providers with current connection status and auth mode for the user.

parameters.name: profileId
parameters.description: Connection profile ID (defaults to user's default profile)

post.description: Initiates OAuth authorization flow (OAuth2 or OAuth1 depending on provider). Returns `authUrl` to redirect the user. If `profileId` is provided, it must belong to the authenticated actor (returns 403 otherwise).
Save an API key credential for a provider that uses api_key auth mode. If `profileId` is provided, it must belong to the authenticated actor (returns 403 otherwise).

profileId.description: Connection profile ID (defaults to user's default profile)

post.description: Save generic credentials for a provider that uses basic or custom auth mode. Fields depend on provider's credential schema. If `profileId` is provided, it must belong to the authenticated actor (returns 403 otherwise).

credentials.description: Credential fields matching the provider's credential schema

profileId.description: Connection profile ID (defaults to user's default profile)

get.description: OAuth callback handler. Supports both OAuth2 (code+state) and OAuth1 (oauth_token+oauth_verifier). Exchanges tokens and closes the popup window.

delete.description: Remove a connection for a provider. If `connectionId` is provided, deletes only that specific connection. Otherwise, deletes all connections for the provider on the profile. The resolved profile must belong to the authenticated actor (returns 403 otherwise).

parameters.name: profileId
parameters.description: Connection profile ID (defaults to user's default profile)

parameters.name: connectionId
parameters.description: Specific connection ID to delete. When provided, only this connection is removed (ignores profileId).

get.description: List all provider configurations (built-in + custom) for the organization. Available to all org members.

post.description: Create a new provider configuration.

put.description: Update a custom provider configuration. Built-in providers cannot be modified.

delete.description: Delete a custom provider. Built-in providers cannot be deleted. Cannot delete if used by agents.

delete.description: Remove admin credentials for a provider.

credentials.description: Key-value pairs matching the provider's adminCredentialSchema fields. Optional for non-OAuth providers.

enabled.description: Whether to enable this provider for use

invalidateConnections.description: When true and credentials are provided, all existing user connections for this provider in the org are deleted. Users will need to reconnect.

List permission scopes available for API key creation, based on the current user's role.

scopes.description: Permission scopes the current user can assign to API keys

get.description: List active (non-revoked) API keys for the organization.

post.description: Create a new API key. The raw key is returned **once** in the response and cannot be retrieved later.

schema.description: The API key is scoped to the application specified by the X-App-Id header.

expiresAt.description: ISO 8601 datetime. Must be in the future if provided.

scopes.description: Permission scopes for the key (e.g. `agents:read`, `agents:run`). Omit or pass empty array for full role access. Invalid or unauthorized scopes are silently filtered.

201.description: API key created. The `key` field contains the raw key (shown only once).

key.description: Raw API key (prefix: ask_). Store it securely — it will not be shown again.

scopes.description: Validated scopes granted to the key. Empty = full role access.

delete.description: Revoke (soft-delete) an API key. The key will immediately stop working.

get.description: List organizations the current user is a member of.

get.description: Container-to-host only. Auth via Bearer run token.

example.title: Forbidden
example.detail: Run is not in running state

post.description: Called by sidecar on upstream 401 to force an OAuth2 token refresh before retrying. If the provider is not OAuth2 or has no refresh token, returns current credentials unchanged. If the refresh itself fails, flags the connection as needs_reconnection and returns 401. Container-to-host only. Auth via Bearer run token.
Called by sidecar when an upstream provider returns 401. Flags the connection as needs_reconnection. Container-to-host only. Auth via Bearer run token.
Set display name after invitation signup.

200.description: Unread counts keyed by package ID

counts.description: Map of packageId to unread notification count

put.description: Marks the notification for a specific run as read.
Marks all unread notifications as read for the current user.

updated.description: Number of notifications marked as read

get.description: Lists all runs for the organization across all agents, ordered by most recent. Use `?user=me` to filter to the current user's runs only.

parameters.name: user
parameters.description: Filter runs by user. `me` returns only the current user's runs. Omit for all org runs.

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Maximum number of runs to return

post.description: Import a package (agent, skill, tool, or provider) from a ZIP file. The ZIP must contain a valid manifest.json. The package scope does not need to match your organization — cross-org packages are imported read-only (fork to modify). Rate-limited to 10 requests/minute. Returns 409 if the target package has unpublished draft changes — re-submit with ?force=true to overwrite.

parameters.name: force
parameters.description: Skip draft overwrite protection. Set to true to overwrite a package with unpublished changes.

409.description: Package has unpublished draft changes that would be overwritten, or version exists with different content

post.summary: Import a package from a GitHub URL
post.description: Import a package (agent, skill, tool, or provider) from a public GitHub repository URL. The URL must point to a directory containing a valid manifest.json. The package scope does not need to match your organization — cross-org packages are imported read-only (fork to modify). Rate-limited to 10 requests/minute.

url.description: GitHub URL pointing to a repository or subdirectory (e.g. https://github.com/owner/repo/tree/main/path)

400.description: Validation error or GitHub import error (invalid URL, repo too large, rate limited, etc.)

409.description: Package has unpublished draft changes that would be overwritten, or version exists with different content

get.description: Download a specific version of a package as a ZIP file. Supports exact version, dist-tag, or semver range resolution. Rate-limited to 50 requests/minute.

parameters.name: version
parameters.description: Exact version, dist-tag (e.g. 'latest'), or semver range (e.g. '^1.0.0')

200.description: ZIP file with integrity and disposition headers

X-Integrity.description: SHA256 SRI hash of the artifact

X-Yanked.description: Present and set to 'true' if the version is yanked

List all published versions for a skill.

version.description: Optional semver version override (e.g. from bump selector)

delete.description: Permanently delete a skill version. Reassigns affected dist-tags to the next best stable version.

error.description: IN_USE: referenced by agents. DEPENDED_ON: required by other packages.

dependents.description: Packages depending on this skill (for DEPENDED_ON)

Returns all published versions for an agent.

post.summary: Create an agent version from draft
post.description: Create an immutable version snapshot. Version is determined by the manifest version field unless overridden. Requires no running runs.

version.description: Optional semver version override (e.g. from bump selector)

post.summary: Restore an agent version into the draft
post.description: Restore a published version into the draft. Requires no runs in progress.

get.description: Returns the detail of a specific agent version including manifest and content.

delete.description: Permanently delete an agent version. Reassigns affected dist-tags to the next best stable version. Blocked if runs are in progress.

post.summary: Fork a package to your organization
post.description: Create an editable copy of a non-owned package under the current organization's scope. The fork is based on the latest published version of the source package — the version manifest, content, and ZIP are copied. A local published version is automatically created. Returns 400 if the source has no published version.

name.description: Custom name for the forked package (slug format). Defaults to the source package name.

packageId.description: New package ID under org scope

400.description: Already owned, name collision, invalid name, or no published version

get.description: List all provider packages (system + org) in the organization.

post.description: Create a new provider package from manifest and content. Creates an initial version automatically.

get.summary: Get version info for a provider package (latest published + draft)
get.description: Returns the latest published version and the current draft version from the manifest.
List all published versions for a provider package.

post.description: Create an immutable version snapshot from the current provider package draft. Version is determined by the manifest version field unless overridden.

version.description: Optional semver version override (e.g. from bump selector)

post.description: Restore a previously published version into the provider package draft. Does not create a new version.

parameters.name: version
parameters.description: Version to restore (exact, dist-tag, or semver range)

parameters.name: version
parameters.description: Version query — exact version, dist-tag, or semver range

post.description: Create a webhook endpoint. The secret is returned once in the response. Max 20 webhooks per org.

packageId.description: Filter by agent ID (null = all agents)

payloadMode.description: Payload mode: full includes result/input, summary omits them

201.description: Webhook created. The `secret` field is shown only once.

secret.description: Webhook secret (whsec_ prefix). Store securely — shown only once.

get.description: List all webhooks for the current application (resolved from X-App-Id header or API key).
Get a single webhook by ID.

put.description: Update webhook URL, events, filters, or enabled status. Cannot change the secret.

delete.description: Delete a webhook and all its delivery history.

post.description: Send a synthetic test.ping event to verify webhook connectivity.
Generate a new secret. The previous secret remains valid for 24 hours (grace period). During rotation, signatures are emitted with both secrets.

get.description: List recent delivery attempts for a webhook (status, latency, response code).

post.description: Create a new application for the organization. Applications scope end-users and their sessions.

allowedRedirectDomains.description: Allowed OAuth redirect domains (e.g. myapp.com, staging.myapp.com). Subdomains are matched automatically.

get.description: List all applications for the organization.
Get a single application by ID.

allowedRedirectDomains.description: Allowed OAuth redirect domains (e.g. myapp.com, staging.myapp.com). Subdomains are matched automatically.

delete.description: Delete an application and all associated end-users. The default application cannot be deleted.

get.description: List all packages installed in this application, with their config and version.

post.description: Install a package from the organization catalog into this application.

409.description: Package already installed in this application

put.description: Update configuration, model/proxy overrides, or version pinning for an installed package.

delete.description: Remove a package from this application.

get.description: List all application-level provider credential overrides and enablement status.

put.description: Set or update application-level provider admin credentials (e.g. OAuth clientId/clientSecret). These override org-level credentials for this application. The provider must be enabled at org level.

credentials.description: Admin credentials (e.g. clientId, clientSecret for OAuth providers)

enabled.description: Whether the provider is enabled for this application. Can only restrict (disable), not override an org-level disable.

delete.description: Remove the application-level override for a provider, reverting to org-level credentials.

post.description: Create a new end-user within an application. At least one of name, email, or externalId should be provided for identification.

externalId.description: Your system's unique identifier for this end-user

metadata.description: Key-value metadata. Max 50 keys, key length 1–40 chars, values: string (max 500), number, boolean, or null.

get.description: List end-users with cursor-based pagination. Filter by applicationId, externalId, or email.

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Maximum number of end-users to return

parameters.name: startingAfter
parameters.description: Cursor for forward pagination (end-user ID to start after)

parameters.name: endingBefore
parameters.description: Cursor for backward pagination (end-user ID to end before)

hasMore.description: Whether more results exist beyond this page

limit.description: The limit that was applied to this query

get.description: Get a single end-user by ID.

patch.description: Update end-user name, email, externalId, or metadata.

externalId.description: Your system's unique identifier for this end-user

metadata.description: Key-value metadata. Max 50 keys, key length 1–40 chars, values: string (max 500), number, boolean, or null.

delete.description: Permanently delete an end-user.

post.summary: Enable end-user auth for an application
post.description: Creates an OAuth client for the application, enabling end-user OIDC authentication. Returns the client credentials (clientId and clientSecret). The clientSecret is only shown once.

redirectUris.description: Allowed redirect URIs for the OAuth flow

allowSignup.description: Whether end-users can sign up via the OIDC flow

requireEmailVerification.description: Whether end-users must verify their email before access

409.description: End-user auth already enabled for this application

get.summary: Get end-user auth config for an application
get.description: Returns the current end-user auth configuration for an application. If not enabled, returns `{ enabled: false }`.

patch.description: Update end-user auth settings for an application. End-user auth must be enabled first via POST.

delete.summary: Disable end-user auth for an application
delete.description: Disables end-user OIDC authentication for the application.

cookieAuth.name: better-auth.session_token
cookieAuth.description: Cookie session from Better Auth. Requires `X-Org-Id` header for org-scoped routes.

bearerApiKey.description: API key authentication. Use `Authorization: Bearer ask_...` header. The org is resolved from the key — no `X-Org-Id` header needed.

bearerExecToken.description: Run token for container-to-host internal routes.

XOrgId.name: X-Org-Id
XOrgId.description: Organization ID. Required for cookie auth. Not needed for API key auth (org resolved from key).

SseOrgId.name: orgId
SseOrgId.description: Organization ID. Required for SSE auth (cookies cannot carry X-Org-Id header on EventSource).

Verbose.name: verbose
Verbose.description: When true, include full payload with `result` and `data` fields. Default (false) strips large user-content fields for safer consumption by external agents.

AppstrateUser.name: Appstrate-User
AppstrateUser.description: End-user ID (eu_ prefix) to execute the request on behalf of. API key auth only — rejected with 400 on cookie auth.

AppstrateVersion.name: Appstrate-Version
AppstrateVersion.description: API version override (format: YYYY-MM-DD). Defaults to the org's pinned version or the current platform version.

IdempotencyKey.name: Idempotency-Key
IdempotencyKey.description: Unique key for idempotent requests (max 255 chars). Prevents duplicate resource creation on retries. Cached for 24 hours.

SseAppId.name: appId
SseAppId.description: Application ID. Required for cookie auth (SSE cannot send X-App-Id header). Not needed for API key auth (app resolved from key).

SseToken.name: token
SseToken.description: API key (ask_ prefix) for SSE authentication. EventSource cannot send Authorization headers, so API key auth uses this query parameter instead.

XAppId.name: X-App-Id
XAppId.description: Application ID. Required for app-scoped routes (agents, runs, schedules, webhooks). Not needed for API key auth (app resolved from key).

RequestId.description: Unique request identifier (req_ prefix). Included on every response for tracing.

AppstrateVersion.description: API version used for this request (format: YYYY-MM-DD). Always included on authenticated responses.

Sunset.description: RFC 8594 Sunset header. Present when the requested API version is deprecated, indicating when it will be removed.

IdempotentReplayed.description: Set to 'true' when the response is a cached replay of a previous idempotent request.

RateLimit.description: IETF RateLimit structured header (limit=N, remaining=M, reset=S). Present on rate-limited endpoints.

RateLimitPolicy.description: IETF RateLimit-Policy header describing the rate limit window (e.g. 20;w=60).

RetryAfter.description: Seconds to wait before retrying. Present on 429 responses.

ProblemDetail.description: RFC 9457 Problem Details for HTTP APIs

title.description: Short summary of the error type

instance.description: URI reference identifying this specific occurrence

ApplicationPackage.description: A package installed in an application with its config and overrides.

applicationId.description: Application ID (app_ prefix). All webhooks are application-scoped.

apiVersion.description: Pinned API version for this organization (format: YYYY-MM-DD). Automatically set to the current version at org creation. New API versions do not affect existing orgs until explicitly updated.

source.description: How the connection profile was resolved

profileName.description: Name of the connection profile used

profileOwnerName.description: Name of the owner of the connection profile

config.description: AFPS schema wrapper for agent configuration (set once, reused across runs).

input.description: AFPS schema wrapper for per-run input.

output.description: AFPS schema wrapper for per-run output.

lastRun.description: Summary of the most recent run (null if never run)

versionCount.description: Number of published versions (0 for built-in agents)

agentAppProfileId.description: Admin-configured app connection profile ID (null if none)

agentAppProfileName.description: Display name of the admin-configured app connection profile

hasUnarchivedChanges.description: Whether the active version has changes not yet archived as a version

populatedProviders.description: ProviderConfig keyed by provider ID for the agent's required providers

callbackUrl.description: OAuth callback URL for provider connections

yanked.description: Whether this version has been yanked

versionDirty.description: Whether the draft had unpublished changes at run time

proxyLabel.description: Proxy label used at run time

modelLabel.description: Model label used at run time

endUserId.description: End-user ID (eu_ prefix) if executed on behalf of an end-user

applicationId.description: Application ID (app_ prefix) that owns this run

metadata.description: Additional metadata (e.g. creditsUsed in cloud mode)

level.description: Log severity level. Non-admin users only receive info, warn, and error logs.

hasCredentials.description: Whether admin credentials are currently configured for this provider

enabled.description: Whether this provider is enabled for use in the organization

adminCredentialSchema.description: JSON Schema describing admin credential fields. Undefined means no admin credentials needed.

credentialSchema.description: JSON Schema for custom credential fields
JSON Schema for custom credential fields

keyPrefix.description: First 8 chars of the key for identification

scopes.description: Permission scopes granted to this API key.

manifestName.description: Manifest name (@scope/name) — may differ from package ID

hasUnarchivedChanges.description: Whether the active version has changes not yet archived as a version

providerKeyId.description: Provider key ID for API key credentials

isDefault.description: Whether this is the default application

allowedRedirectDomains.description: Domains allowed for OAuth redirect callbacks

createdBy.description: ID of the user who created the application

emailVerified.description: Whether the end-user's email address has been verified

AgentManifest.description: AFPS Agent manifest extended with Appstrate platform fields. Standard fields are defined by the AFPS Agent schema; extension fields use the x- prefix per AFPS §10.

SkillManifest.description: AFPS Skill manifest. See https://afps.appstrate.dev for field reference.

ToolManifest.description: AFPS Tool manifest. See https://afps.appstrate.dev for field reference.

ProviderManifest.description: AFPS Provider manifest. See https://afps.appstrate.dev for field reference.

FileConstraintsMap.description: Upload constraints for file fields, keyed by property name. Lives at the AFPS wrapper level (outside the JSON Schema).

UIHintsMap.description: UI rendering hints for schema fields, keyed by property name. Lives at the AFPS wrapper level (outside the JSON Schema).

placeholder.description: Hint text shown before the user provides a value

example.title: Rate Limited
example.detail: Too many requests. Please try again shortly.

IdempotencyInProgress.description: A request with the same Idempotency-Key is already being processed

example.title: Idempotency In Progress
example.detail: A request with the same Idempotency-Key is already being processed. Please wait and retry.

example.title: Internal Server Error
example.detail: An unexpected error occurred. Please try again or contact support.

IdempotencyConflict.description: Same Idempotency-Key used with a different request body

example.title: Idempotency Conflict
example.detail: This Idempotency-Key was already used with a different request body. Use a new key for different requests.
